spec_match.rationale: Simulates source.attribution_scopes.{values,limit} vs single trigger scope membership, which is the core of the spec, but ignores max_event_states, scope budget exhaustion, and the case where the trigger has multiple scopes (the spec allows trigger.attribution_scopes to be a list with set-intersection semantics).

interactive.rationale: Toggle chips for source scopes, radio-style chip for trigger scope, live numeric input for limit, all driving a deterministic match readout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises client-side simulation of the new attribution_scopes field — page delivers it.

edge_cases.rationale: No handling of: limit < scopes.length (the truncation behaviour), empty source scopes (sources without scopes always match), trigger with multiple scopes, scope_budget exhaustion via max_event_states. The page collapses the trigger to a single scope which is the easy path only.

references.rationale: Links chromestatus.com/feature/5096560068395008 plus the WICG explainer.

design_system.rationale: All CSS variables, scope-chip override sets both background and color, --accent-amber used on warn block which exists in styles.css.

openQuestions.title: Add multi-scope trigger support
openQuestions.detail: The spec allows trigger.attribution_scopes to be an array; attribution proceeds if any trigger scope intersects any source scope. The current UI forces the trigger to a single chip (radio-like behaviour), which misses the headline 'set-intersection' framing. Convert the trigger row to multi-select chips like the source row and surface the intersection size in the result.

openQuestions.title: Show what truncation does when limit < scopes.length
openQuestions.detail: Right now if the source has 4 scopes and limit=3 the simulator silently drops the 4th from the effective set. That's the spec behaviour but the visitor has no way to know it happened — annotate the dropped scope visually (greyed-out chip plus an 'over scope_limit, ignored' badge).

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the empty-scopes branch
openQuestions.detail: A source registered WITHOUT attribution_scopes still matches every trigger (legacy behaviour preserved for backwards compatibility). Add a 'no scopes' chip that clears the source set and shows the trigger always attributes — that's the obvious teaching moment about when to opt in.
